title: Introduction to the mtarm Package
text: mtarm package provides computational tool designed Bayesian estimation, inference, forecasting multivariate Threshold Autoregressive (TAR) models. models provide versatile approach modeling nonlinear multivariate time series include multivariate Self-Exciting Threshold Autoregressive (SETAR) Vector Autoregressive (VAR) models particular cases (Vanegas, Calderón V, Rondón 2025). package accommodates broad class innovation distributions beyond Gaussian assumption, Student-tt, slash, symmetric hyperbolic, Laplace, contaminated normal, skew-normal, skew-tt distributions, thereby enabling robust modeling heavy tails, asymmetry, non-Gaussian characteristics.

title: Introduction to the mtarm Package
code: remotes::install_github("lhvanegasp/mtarm")

title: Introduction to the mtarm Package
code: install.packages("mtarm")

title: Introduction to the mtarm Package
text: data available object `iceland.rf` obtained (Tong 1990), provided detailed description geographical meteorological characteristics rivers analyzed series individually. Subsequently, (Tsay 1998) conducted bivariate analysis dataset. focus bivariate time series {(Y1,t,Y2,t)⊤}t≥1\{(Y_{1,t},Y_{2,t})^{\top}\}_{t\geq 1}, Y1,tY_{1,t} Y2,tY_{2,t} denote daily river flow (cubic meters per second, m3/s{m}^3/{s}) Jökulsá Eystri Vatnsdalsá rivers, respectively. sample covers period 1972 1974, comprising 1095 observations. exogenous variables include daily precipitation XtX_t, measured millimeters (mm{mm}), temperature ZtZ_t, measured degrees Celsius (∘C^\circ\mathrm{C}), recorded meteorological station Hveravellir. Precipitation corresponds accumulated rainfall 9:00 .M. previous day 9:00 .M. current day.

title: Introduction to the mtarm Package
text: Following (Tsay 1998), series modeled using TAR(2;p=(15,15),q=(4,4),d=(2,2))\mathrm{TAR}(2; p=(15,15), q=(4,4), d=(2,2)) specification given $$Y_t=\sum\limits_{j=1}^2 (Z_{t-h}\(c_{j-1},c_j])\Big(\!{\phi}_0^{^{(j)}}+\sum\limits_{=1}^{15}\boldsymbol{\phi}_i^{^{(j)}}Y_{t-}+\sum\limits_{=1}^{4}\boldsymbol{\beta}_i^{^{(j)}}\!X_{t-}+\sum\limits_{=1}^{2}{\delta}_i^{^{(j)}}\!Z_{t-}+\epsilon_t^{^{(j)}}\!\Big),$$ ϵt(j)\epsilon_t^{^{(j)}} error term. last 55 observations (November 7 December 31, 1974), corresponding 5%5\% sample, excluded estimation stage reserved --sample forecast evaluation. following code requests estimation TAR(2;p=(15,15),q=(4,4),d=(2,2))\mathrm{TAR}(2; p=(15,15), q=(4,4), d=(2,2)) specification Gaussian, Slash, Student-tt, Laplace error distributions.

code: fits <- mtar_grid(~ Jokulsa + Vatnsdalsa | Temperature | Precipitation,                   data=iceland.rf, subset={Date<="1974-11-06"},                                              row.names=Date, nregim.min=2, nregim.max=2, p.min=15,                                    p.max=15, q.min=4, q.max=4, d.min=2, d.max=2,                                              n.burnin=200, n.sim=300, n.thin=3, ssvs=TRUE,                   dist=c("Gaussian","Slash","Student-t","Laplace"),                   plan_strategy="multisession")  fits #>  #>  #> Sample size          :1026 time points (1972-01-16 to 1974-11-06) #>  #> Output Series        :Jokulsa    |    Vatnsdalsa #>  #> Threshold Series (TS):Temperature with a estimated delay equal to 0 #>  #> Exogenous Series (ES):Precipitation #>  #> Error Distribution   :Gaussian #>  #> Number of regimes    :2 to 2 #>  #> Deterministics       :Intercept #>  #> Autoregressive orders:15 to 15 #>  #> Maximum lags for ES  :4 to 4 #>  #> Maximum lags for TS  :2 to 2

title: Auxiliary function to specify the number of regimes and lag orders — ars
text: nregim positive integer indicating total number regimes. p list positive integers specifying autoregressive order output series within regime. q list non-negative integers specifying maximum lag exogenous series within regime. d list non-negative integers specifying maximum lag threshold series within regime.

title: Auxiliary function to specify the number of regimes and lag orders — ars
text: list containing number regimes regime-specific lag-order specifications.

what: Geweke's convergence diagnostic for mtar objects — geweke_diagTAR
title: Geweke's convergence diagnostic for mtar objects — geweke_diagTAR
text: function computes Geweke's convergence diagnostic Markov chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) output obtained Bayesian estimation multivariate TAR models. wrapper around geweke.diag() applies diagnostic posterior chains returned call mtar().

title: Geweke's convergence diagnostic for mtar objects — geweke_diagTAR
code: geweke_diagTAR(x, frac1 = 0.1, frac2 = 0.5)

title: Geweke's convergence diagnostic for mtar objects — geweke_diagTAR
text: x object class mtar returned function mtar(). frac1 numeric value \((0,1)\) specifying fraction initial part chain used diagnostic. frac2 numeric value \((0,1)\) specifying fraction final part chain used diagnostic.

title: Geweke's convergence diagnostic for mtar objects — geweke_diagTAR
text: list containing Geweke z-scores parameters mtar model.

what: Bayesian estimation of a multivariate Threshold Autoregressive (TAR) model. — mtar
title: Bayesian estimation of a multivariate Threshold Autoregressive (TAR) model. — mtar
text: function implements Gibbs sampling algorithm generate draws posterior distribution parameters multivariate Threshold Autoregressive (TAR) model, including special cases SETAR VAR models. approach accommodates wide range noise process distributions, including Gaussian, Student-\(t\), Slash, symmetric hyperbolic, contaminated normal, Laplace, skew-normal, skew-Student-\(t\).

title: Bayesian estimation of a multivariate Threshold Autoregressive (TAR) model. — mtar
code: mtar(   formula,   data,   subset,   Intercept = TRUE,   trend = c("none", "linear", "quadratic"),   nseason = NULL,   ars = ars(),   row.names,   dist = c("Gaussian", "Student-t", "Hyperbolic", "Laplace", "Slash",     "Contaminated normal", "Skew-Student-t", "Skew-normal"),   prior = list(),   n.sim = 500,   n.burnin = 100,   n.thin = 1,   ssvs = FALSE,   setar = NULL,   progress = TRUE,   ... )

title: Bayesian estimation of a multivariate Threshold Autoregressive (TAR) model. — mtar
text: formula three-part expression class Formula describing TAR model fitted. first part specifies variables multivariate output series, second part defines threshold series, third part specifies variables multivariate exogenous series. data data frame containing variables model. found data, variables taken environment(formula), typically environment mtar_grid() called. subset optional vector specifying subset observations used fitting process. Intercept optional logical indicating whether intercept included within regime. trend optional character string specifying degree deterministic time trend included regime. Available options "linear", "quadratic", "none". default, trend set "none". nseason optional integer, greater equal 2, specifying number seasonal periods. provided, nseason - 1 seasonal dummy variables added regressors within regime. default, nseason set NULL, thereby indicating TAR model seasonal effects. ars list defining autoregressive structure model. contains four components: number regimes (nregim), autoregressive order within regime (p), maximum lags exogenous (q) threshold (d) series regime. object can validated using helper function ars(). row.names optional variable data labelling time points corresponding row data set. dist character string specifying multivariate distributions used model noise process. Available options "Gaussian", "Student-t", "Slash", "Hyperbolic", "Laplace", "Contaminated normal", "Skew-normal", "Skew-Student-t". default, dist set "Gaussian". prior optional list specifying hyperparameter values define prior distribution. list can validated using priors() function. default, prior set empty list, thereby indicating hyperparameter values set non-informative prior distribution obtained. n.sim optional positive integer specifying number simulation iterations burn-period. default, n.sim set 500. n.burnin optional positive integer specifying number burn-iterations. default, n.burnin set 100. n.thin optional positive integer specifying thinning interval. default, n.thin set 1. ssvs optional logical indicating whether Stochastic Search Variable Selection (SSVS) procedure applied identify relevant lags output, exogenous, threshold series. default, ssvs set FALSE. setar optional positive integer indicating component output series used threshold variable. default, setar set NULL, indicating fitted model SETAR model. progress optional logical indicating whether progress bar displayed execution. default, progress set TRUE. ... arguments passed methods.

title: Simulation of multivariate time series from a TAR model — simtar
code: simtar(   n,   k = 2,   ars = ars(),   Intercept = TRUE,   trend = c("none", "linear", "quadratic"),   nseason = NULL,   parms,   delay = 0,   thresholds = NULL,   t.series = NULL,   ex.series = NULL,   dist = c("Gaussian", "Student-t", "Hyperbolic", "Laplace", "Slash",     "Contaminated normal", "Skew-Student-t", "Skew-normal"),   skewness = NULL,   extra = NULL,   setar = NULL,   Verbose = TRUE )

title: Simulation of multivariate time series from a TAR model — simtar
text: n positive integer specifying length simulated output series. k positive integer specifying dimension multivariate output series. ars list defining TAR model structure, composed four elements: number regimes (nregim), autoregressive order (p), maximum lags exogenous (q) threshold (d) series within regime. object can validated using ars() function. Intercept optional logical indicating whether intercept term included regime. trend optional character string specifying degree deterministic time trend included regime. Available options linear trend ("linear"), quadratic trend ("quadratic"), trend ("none"). default, trend set "none". nseason optional integer, greater equal 2, specifying number seasonal periods. provided, nseason - 1 seasonal dummy variables added regressors within regime. default, nseason set NULL, thereby indicating TAR model seasonal effects. parms list one sublist per regime. sublist contains two matrices: first matrix corresponds location parameters, second matrix corresponds scale parameters model. delay optional non-negative integer specifying delay parameter threshold series. default, delay set 0. thresholds numeric vector length nregim-1 containing threshold values, sorted ascending order. t.series matrix containing values threshold series. ex.series matrix containing values multivariate exogenous series. dist optional character string specifying multivariate distribution chosen model noise process. Supported options include Gaussian ("Gaussian"), Student-\(t\) ("Student-t"), Slash ("Slash"), Symmetric Hyperbolic ("Hyperbolic"), Laplace ("Laplace"), Contaminated Normal ("Contaminated normal"). default, dist set "Gaussian". skewness optional numeric vector specifying skewness parameters noise distribution, applicable. extra optional value specifying extra parameter noise distribution, required. setar optional positive integer indicating component output series used threshold variable. default, setar set NULL, indicating model SETAR type. Verbose optional logical indicating whether description simulated TAR model printed. default, Verbose set TRUE.
